title: If local storage file exists, then use it rather then defau…
body:
…lts.

Currently we always force overlay if it exists even though a user might want
vfs.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: /approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@giuseppe PTAL

hm.. doesn't ReloadConfigurationFile override the setting in storageOpts?

AFAICS, if the storage driver is specified in the local configration then it is used.  overlay is used only when the driver is not specified and `fuse-overlayfs` is present.

@giuseppe the issues is the storage options continue to be used.  
```
 ./bin/podman info | grep overlay
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  - overlay.mount_program=/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  - overlay.mount_program=/bin/fuse-overlayfs
```
Notice it has fuse-overlay twice.
Now I want to change my storage to vfs.

```
 $ cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf 
[storage]
  driver = "vfs"
  runroot = "/run/user/3267"
  graphroot = "/home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers/storage"
 $ ./bin/podman info | grep overlay
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  - overlay.mount_program=/bin/fuse-overlayfs
```
So it ignores my local storage.conf

I think this depends on a recent change where if the configuration is different than what we have in the storage database, then we take the storage database version instead of erroring out.  In facts, also with this patch I could not see any difference in the info output.

To see the change reflected, after the configuration is changed, you will also need to `rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers/`.

I removed ~/.local/share/containers ~/.config/containers
podman info shows
```
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  - overlay.mount_program=/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /run/user/3267
```

I change
```
 cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf 
[storage]
  driver = "vfs"
  runroot = "/run/user/3267"
  graphroot = "/home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers/storage"
```
podman info shows
```
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /run/user/3267
```

I think the `podman info` already created the storage.

Could you `rm -rf  ~/.local/share/containers ~/.config/containers` after you change the configuration files?

Well while that shows that this is a total waste of time, since there is no way a user would do this, I still see issues.

```
sudo rm -rf ~dwalsh/.config/containers/ ~dwalsh/.local/share/containers/
$ mkdir ~/.config/containers
$ cat > ~/.config/containers/storage.conf << _EOF
[storage]
  driver = "vfs"
  runroot = "/run/user/3267"
  graphroot = "/home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers/storage"
_EOF
podman info
...
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /run/user/3267
```

Now shows vfs but has overlay override.
we could set the `mount_program` only after we read the configuration file and the driver is overlay.
@giuseppe What is your fear of not setting it?  If a user specifies overlay in the storage.conf but does not specify the mount, we should throw an error,  rather then assuming they want it?

> @giuseppe What is your fear of not setting it? If a user specifies overlay in the storage.conf but does not specify the mount, we should throw an error, rather then assuming they want it?

now that we are using overlay + fuse-overlayfs by default, if we don't set the fuse-overlayfs mount path then podman will stop working ouf of the box.  By setting the mount path, podman for new users just work with overlay without any configuration tweak.
Yes I agree.   This is just about people who actually modify the storage.conf file.  We should not be modifying what they say they want.

ok let's go with this then, as anyway once we have multiple storage/runtime support this problem won't exist at all :-)

LGTM
@baude @mheon @TomSweeneyRedHat @vrothberg PTAL
LGTM
We need to verify this does not break existing users on upgrade - someone should install podman 0.10.x and upgrade to a version with this patch and verify that everything still works
/lgtm

@rhatdan: you cannot LGTM your own PR.
